title: Methods for finite blocklength coding in wireless communication systems
abstract: Methods, apparatuses and systems for determining a channel quality indicator (CQI) and adjusting a modulation and coding scheme (MCS) for a downlink (DL) transmission are provided. In particular, a method implemented in a wireless transmit/receive unit (WTRU) includes receiving control information, including a codeword blocklength and a target BLER parameter for the DL transmission, measuring channel state information (CSI), determining a WTRU-based CQI based on the measured CSI, the codeword blocklength, and the target BLER parameter, and transmitting the WTRU-based CQI to a base station. The method may further include receiving a network-based CQI for the DL transmission, the network-based CQI being derived from the WTRU-based CQI, determining an MCS based on the network-based CQI, and receiving the DL transmission using the determined MCS.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed: 1. A method implemented in a wireless transmit/receive unit (WTRU), comprising: receiving control information, including a codeword blocklength and a target block error rate (BLER) parameter for a downlink (DL) transmission, wherein the target BLER parameter is a target BLER or a target BLER range; measuring channel state information (CSI); determining a WTRU-based channel quality indicator (CQI) based on the measured CSI, the codeword blocklength, and the target BLER parameter; and transmitting the WTRU-based CQI to a base station. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving a network-based CQI for the DL transmission, wherein the network- based CQI is derived from the WTRU-based CQI; determining a modulation and coding scheme (MCS) based on the network-based CQI; and receiving the DL transmission using the determined MCS. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving resource allocation information, along with the network-based CQI, for receiving the DL transmission according to the network-based CQI. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein: receiving the control information includes receiving a channel code type for the DL transmission, and determining the WTRU-based CQI includes determining the WTRU-based CQI based on the measured CSI, the codeword blocklength, the target BLER parameter, and the channel code type. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the WTRU-based CQI comprises: 

 comparing the codeword blocklength to at least one codeword blocklength threshold; and selecting the WTRU-based CQI from among a plurality of CQI based on a comparison result. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein: on a condition the codeword blocklength is below the codeword blocklength threshold, selecting the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a first modulation and coding scheme (MCS), from among the plurality of CQI; and on a condition the codeword blocklength is equal to or greater than the codeword blocklength threshold, selecting the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a second MCS, from among the plurality of CQI, wherein the second MCS is more robust than the first MCS. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the WTRU-based CQI comprises: comparing the target BLER parameter to at least one BLER threshold; and selecting the WTRU-based CQI from among a plurality of CQI based on a comparison result. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein: on a condition the target BLER parameter is below the BLER threshold, selecting the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a first modulation and coding scheme (MCS), from among the plurality of CQI; and on a condition the target BLER parameter is equal to or greater than the BLER threshold, selecting the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a second MCS, from among the plurality of CQI, wherein the second MCS is more robust than the first MCS. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the control information includes a plurality of target BLER parameters for the DL transmission, the method further comprising: determining a plurality of WTRU-based CQIs based on the measured CSI, the codeword blocklength, and the plurality of target BLER parameters, wherein each of the 

 WTRU-based CQIs corresponds to a different one of the plurality of target BLER parameters; transmitting the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs to the base station; receiving at least one network-based CQI for the DL transmission based on the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs; determining an MCS based on each of the at least one received network-based CQI; and receiving the DL transmission using each determined MCS. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the control information includes a plurality of codeword blocklengths for the DL transmission, the method further comprising: determining a plurality of WTRU-based CQIs based on the measured CSI, the plurality of codeword blocklengths, and the target BLER parameter, wherein each of the WTRU-based CQIs corresponds to a different one of the plurality of codeword blocklengths; transmitting the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs to the base station; receiving at least one network-based CQI for the DL transmission based on the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs; determining an MCS based on each of the at least one received network-based CQI; and receiving the DL transmission using each determined MCS. 11. A wireless transmit/receive unit (WTRU), comprising: a receiver configured to receive control information, including a codeword blocklength and a target block error rate (BLER) parameter for a downlink (DL) transmission, wherein the target BLER parameter is a target BLER or a target BLER range; at least one processor configured to measure channel state information (CSI) and determine a WTRU-based channel quality indicator (CQI) based on the measured CSI, the codeword blocklength, and the target BLER parameter; and a transmitter configured to transmit the WTRU-based CQI to a base station. 12. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein: 

 the receiver is configured to receive a network-based CQI for the DL transmission, wherein the network-based CQI is derived from the WTRU-based CQI; the at least one processor is configured to determine a modulation and coding scheme (MCS) based on the network-based CQI; and the receiver is further configured to receive the DL transmission using the determined MCS. 13. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein: the receiver is configured to receive resource allocation information, along with the network-based CQI, for receiving the DL transmission according to the network-based CQI. 14. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein: the control information includes a channel code type for the DL transmission, and the at least one processor is configured to determine the WTRU-based CQI based on the measured CSI, the codeword blocklength, the target BLER parameter, and the channel code type. 15. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein: the at least one processor is configured to determine the WTRU-based CQI, including comparing the codeword blocklength to at least one codeword blocklength threshold, and selecting the WTRU-based CQI from among a plurality of CQI based on a comparison result. 16. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein: on a condition the codeword blocklength is below the codeword blocklength threshold, the at least one processor is configured to select the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a first modulation and coding scheme (MCS), from among the plurality of CQI; and on a condition the codeword blocklength is equal to or greater than the codeword blocklength threshold, the at least one processor is configured to select the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a second MCS, from among the plurality of CQI, wherein the second MCS is more robust than the first MCS. 17. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein: the at least one processor is configured to determine the WTRU-based CQI, including comparing the target BLER parameter to at least one BLER threshold, and selecting the WTRU-based CQI from among a plurality of CQI based on a comparison result. 18. The WTRU of claim 17, wherein: on a condition the target BLER parameter is below the BLER threshold, the at least one processor is configured to select the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a first modulation and coding scheme (MCS), from among the plurality of CQI ; and on a condition the target BLER parameter is equal to or greater than the BLER threshold, the at least one processor is configured to select the WTRU-based CQI, corresponding to a second MCS, from among the plurality of CQI, wherein the second MCS is more robust than the first MCS. 19. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein the control information includes a plurality of target BLER parameters for the DL transmission, the at least one processor is configured to determine a plurality of WTRU-based CQIs based on the measured CSI, the codeword blocklength, and the plurality of target BLER parameters, wherein each of the WTRU-based CQIs corresponds to a different one of the plurality of target BLER parameters; the transmitter is configured to transmit the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs to the base station; the receiver is configured to receive at least one network-based CQI for the DL transmission based on the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs; the at least one processor is configured to determine an MCS based on each of the at least one received network-based CQI; and the receiver is configured to receive the DL transmission using each determined MCS. 20. The WTRU of claim 11, wherein the control information includes a plurality of codeword blocklengths for the DL transmission, the method further comprising: the at least one processor is configured to determine a plurality of WTRU-based CQIs based on the measured CSI, the plurality of codeword blocklengths, and the target BLER parameter, wherein each of the WTRU-based CQIs corresponds to a different one of the plurality of codeword blocklengths; the transmitter is configured to transmit the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs to the base station; the receiver is configured to receive at least one network-based CQI for the DL transmission based on the plurality of WTRU-based CQIs; the at least one processor is configured to determine an MCS based on each of the at least one received network-based CQI; and the receiver is configured to receive the DL transmission using each determined MCS.
